title: Introduction to Teaching

jokes.text: Any questions? You, Red Hair. I'm not gonna learn names.
jokes.setup: Jeff starts teaching, asks for questions - normal teacher behavior expected
jokes.punchline: Immediately admits he won't learn student names, undermining the teacher-student relationship from the start
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jeff character moment - his arrogance and disinterest in actually teaching. Economical setup and payoff.

jokes.text: Will there be a syllabus? - Is good example of a question. Moving on.
jokes.setup: Student asks legitimate academic question about syllabus
jokes.punchline: Jeff treats it as just an example of question format rather than answering it
jokes.explanation: Shows Jeff's complete unpreparedness and avoidance tactics. Well-structured but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Oh, no spoilers, Ski Cap.
jokes.setup: Student asks about grading - normal academic question
jokes.punchline: Jeff treats grading information like movie spoilers that would ruin the experience
jokes.explanation: Clever absurdist reframing of academic expectations. Very quotable and economical.

jokes.text: Are we human beings or just question-and-answer machines?
jokes.setup: Students asking legitimate questions about class structure
jokes.punchline: Jeff philosophically protests having to answer basic teacher responsibilities
jokes.explanation: Jeff's pseudo-philosophical deflection from actually teaching. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: We're students. And you're a teacher. - Yeah. I'm a teacher.
jokes.setup: Student states the obvious reality of their roles
jokes.punchline: Jeff's dawning realization/horror at his situation, delivered with reluctant acceptance
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective beat showing Jeff's existential dread about teaching.

jokes.text: Hey, look, it's the star of 'Head of the Ass.' - Shut up, Leonard! Nice earring. You look like the road manager for the California Raisins.
jokes.setup: Leonard makes crude joke about Jeff
jokes.punchline: Jeff's oddly specific insult comparing Leonard to California Raisins road manager
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly specific and absurd insult. The California Raisins reference is perfectly random yet somehow apt.

jokes.text: What kind of teacher talks to a student like that?
jokes.setup: Jeff just insulted Leonard with creative cruelty
jokes.punchline: Students point out the obvious - this isn't normal teacher behavior
jokes.explanation: Obvious observation that serves as straight-man reaction to Jeff's inappropriate behavior.

jokes.text: You can have that half of the office, but come spring, that corner is stacked to the rafters with fetal pigs.
jokes.setup: Hickey offering Jeff half the office space - seems like normal workplace courtesy
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of storing fetal pigs in the corner
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hickey character introduction - casually dark and matter-of-fact about disturbing details.

jokes.text: How long have you been... - 15 years. 15 years and counting. 'Temporary gig.' Oh, I like you.
jokes.setup: Jeff says teaching is temporary, Hickey seems to relate
jokes.punchline: Hickey reveals he's been 'temporarily' teaching for 15 years, finds Jeff's naivety amusing
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about career stagnation. Great character work showing Hickey's cynicism and Jeff's future.

jokes.text: Table, resume table mode. - Table, power down. Wait! I got a cup on there.
jokes.setup: Abed has made the table voice-activated like high-tech furniture
jokes.punchline: Table actually responds by moving/changing, nearly spilling someone's drink
jokes.explanation: Classic Abed over-engineering something simple. Good physical comedy setup.

jokes.text: It's Professor Winger! Please don't razz me about being a teacher. Teaching is the most noble profession in the world.
jokes.setup: Jeff asks not to be teased about teaching
jokes.punchline: Immediately overcompensates with obviously false praise about teaching being 'most noble'
jokes.explanation: Shows Jeff's insecurity and tendency to overcompensate when defensive.

jokes.text: He fixes cuckoo clocks for fun.
jokes.setup: Jeff complaining about his office mate Hickey
jokes.punchline: Specific, oddly old-fashioned hobby that perfectly captures Hickey's character
jokes.explanation: Perfect detail that establishes Hickey as anachronistic and meticulous.

jokes.text: I wasn't a real lawyer, remember? I can't just pretend I'm teaching. I'm not Mythbusters.
jokes.setup: Jeff explaining he can't fake being a teacher like he faked being a lawyer
jokes.punchline: Compares pretending to teach to Mythbusters, implying they just mess around and call it science
jokes.explanation: Clever pop culture reference that actually illuminates Jeff's predicament.

jokes.text: Well, Jesus wasn't in 70 movies.
jokes.setup: Shirley complaining about Nicolas Cage course vs. no Jesus in science
jokes.punchline: Jeff's matter-of-fact comparison of Jesus and Nicolas Cage filmographies
jokes.explanation: Brilliant deadpan blasphemy. Perfect economy and unexpectedness. Peak Jeff response.

jokes.text: Thanks, Annie, for one glorious second, I forgot I was a teacher.
jokes.setup: Annie reminds Jeff he needs to prepare to teach
jokes.punchline: Jeff's sarcastic gratitude for being reminded of his horrible situation
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jeff's ongoing denial and self-pity.

jokes.text: Oh! Look at Mr. Smarty Pants!
jokes.setup: Dean excited about Jeff teaching
jokes.punchline: Infantilizing praise that shows Dean's inappropriate relationship with Jeff
jokes.explanation: Pure Dean character moment - inappropriate and childish enthusiasm.

jokes.text: Jeff, do you know Excel? - No. - Well, it looks like you do.
jokes.setup: Dean asks if Jeff knows Excel software
jokes.punchline: Despite Jeff saying no, Dean insists he looks like he does
jokes.explanation: Dean's delusional optimism and projection. Good absurdist logic.

jokes.text: Mr. Nadir drove our TV Studies professor out of his mind by proving that there was an answer to the question 'Who's the Boss?' - Angela.
jokes.setup: Professor mentions Abed's history of solving unsolvable questions
jokes.punchline: Abed matter-of-factly states the answer to 'Who's the Boss?' is Angela
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abed moment - meta-textual analysis delivered with complete confidence. Great callback to classic sitcom.

jokes.text: Promise me you will be careful.
jokes.setup: Professor warning Abed about Nicolas Cage analysis
jokes.punchline: Treating pop culture analysis like dangerous territory requiring extreme caution
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the absurd premise. Foreshadows Abed's breakdown.

jokes.text: I can actually experience this food warm and disgusting.
jokes.setup: Jeff cutting in line as a teacher perk
jokes.punchline: Describes cafeteria food as 'warm and disgusting' - a backhanded compliment
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about cafeteria food quality and temperature.

jokes.text: This line is for food, not for Lionel Richie tickets.
jokes.setup: Buzz confronting Jeff about cutting in line
jokes.punchline: Random Lionel Richie reference as comparison
jokes.explanation: Oddly specific pop culture reference that fits Buzz's character

jokes.text: You know what that means? It means he gets your meatballs. - Ah, please. That's all I have.
jokes.setup: Hickey explaining teacher hierarchy to students
jokes.punchline: Actually takes student's food, student pleads it's all he has
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about institutional power abuse. Shows Hickey's casual cruelty.

jokes.text: The trick is, you gotta show the other baboons you got a bigger, redder ass.
jokes.setup: Hickey explaining cafeteria dynamics
jokes.punchline: Compares students to baboons and uses primate dominance metaphor
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hickey cynicism - dehumanizing students with animal metaphor. Very quotable.

jokes.text: Break into groups, grade each other's papers, and please enjoy the seven-disc edition of Planet Earth.
jokes.setup: Jeff asking for teaching advice
jokes.punchline: Teacher's lazy curriculum is just having students teach themselves while watching nature documentaries
jokes.explanation: Good satire of lazy teaching methods. Planet Earth as educational crutch.

jokes.text: You know the rules, Pelton. Drop the checks and move on.
jokes.setup: Dean trying to interact with teachers
jokes.punchline: Teacher treats Dean like unwelcome management, reducing him to check delivery
jokes.explanation: Shows teacher-administration tension. Dean's diminished status.

jokes.text: He seemed scared to smell that flower but happy to get shot.
jokes.setup: Discussing Nicolas Cage's acting choices
jokes.punchline: Pointing out the illogical emotional responses in his performances
jokes.explanation: Good observation about Cage's contradictory screen behavior.

jokes.text: Robert Downey Jr., good, Jim Belushi, bad. Van Damme, the good kind of bad, Johnny Depp, the bad kind of good.
jokes.setup: Abed explaining actor classification system
jokes.punchline: Creates complex taxonomy with paradoxical categories like 'good kind of bad'
jokes.explanation: Peak Abed - analytical, paradoxical, and weirdly insightful. The Van Damme/Depp classifications are brilliant.

jokes.text: Hey, let's turd some lockers!
jokes.setup: Teachers hanging out, acting unprofessional
jokes.punchline: Hickey suggesting juvenile vandalism using 'turd' as a verb
jokes.explanation: Hickey's regression to juvenile behavior. 'Turd' as verb is unexpectedly funny.

jokes.text: It's 'syllabi.'
jokes.setup: Jeff has been acting unprofessionally with other teachers
jokes.punchline: Suddenly corrects plural of syllabus, showing he does know academic things
jokes.explanation: Nice character beat - Jeff's intelligence peeking through his facade.

jokes.text: Hey, walk, don't run, mister! This isn't a racetrack!
jokes.setup: Jeff trying to act like a real teacher
jokes.punchline: Uses cartoonishly generic teacher reprimand
jokes.explanation: Shows Jeff's artificial attempt at teacher authority using cliched phrases.

jokes.text: Ahh, she in your class, yo!
jokes.setup: Annie enters Jeff's class looking serious
jokes.punchline: Student recognizes this spells trouble for Jeff
jokes.explanation: Simple reaction showing students recognize Annie as Jeff's nemesis.

jokes.text: You don't have to rub it in. - What I saw today was embarrassing.
jokes.setup: Jeff thinking Annie is going to be supportive
jokes.punchline: Annie immediately launches into criticism
jokes.explanation: Good character dynamic - Jeff's hope immediately crushed by Annie's directness.

jokes.text: When I asked you to explain the Sixth Amendment, you pled the Fifth! - I know my rights.
jokes.setup: Annie pointing out Jeff's legal ignorance
jokes.punchline: Jeff completely misses the irony and thinks he made a clever legal move
jokes.explanation: Brilliant legal wordplay showing Jeff's ignorance. His confident response makes it even funnier.

jokes.text: I wish Troy was in my class instead of you!
jokes.setup: Jeff frustrated with Annie's criticism
jokes.punchline: Wishes for his absent friend instead - inappropriate teacher behavior
jokes.explanation: Shows Jeff's immaturity and references Troy's departure from the show.

jokes.text: I hate her, I hate her, I hate her, I hate her, I hate her, I hate her, I hate her, I hate her, I hate her.
jokes.setup: Jeff upset with Annie
jokes.punchline: Childish repetition shows complete emotional regression
jokes.explanation: Jeff's complete emotional breakdown into child-like behavior.

jokes.text: She's lead, we're chalk.
jokes.setup: Hickey giving Jeff advice about dealing with Annie
jokes.punchline: Plays on 'lead' (leadership/student) vs 'chalk' (teachers/blackboard)
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay connecting student materials to power dynamics. Very economical.

jokes.text: Give her A-minuses. It'll drive her nuts, she'll drop.
jokes.setup: Hickey suggesting how to get rid of Annie
jokes.punchline: Psychological warfare through grade manipulation
jokes.explanation: Dark but clever understanding of Annie's perfectionism. Sets up major plot point.

jokes.text: Teachers don't have to explain minuses. Why do you think we invented 'em?
jokes.setup: Jeff worried Annie will call him on unfair grading
jokes.punchline: Hickey reveals minus grades exist purely for teacher power
jokes.explanation: Brilliant cynical insight into academic power structures. Very quotable.

jokes.text: You like the Leonard Meatballs? They added something special. - It's Leonard.
jokes.setup: Hickey asking about cafeteria meatballs having special ingredient
jokes.punchline: The special ingredient is literally Leonard - implying cannibalism
jokes.explanation: Shockingly dark humor. Perfect callback to earlier Leonard scene. Economical and unexpected.

jokes.text: Go get your earring, you piece of human garbage.
jokes.setup: Leonard asking for his earring back after the meatball revelation
jokes.punchline: Hickey's casual, vicious insult delivered matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Hickey's casual cruelty perfectly delivered. The contrast with his helpful tone is jarring.

jokes.text: Maybe Nicolas Cage is just... crazy. - All actors are crazy, Annie.
jokes.setup: Annie suggesting simple explanation for Cage
jokes.punchline: Abed dismisses this as too obvious - all actors are crazy
jokes.explanation: Good Abed logic - dismissing obvious answer because it's not analytical enough.

jokes.text: I think you're telling us we should teach ourselves. - I don't think you're gonna learn if I tell you how to think.
jokes.setup: Student calling out Jeff's lazy teaching method
jokes.punchline: Jeff spins his laziness as philosophical pedagogy
jokes.explanation: Classic Jeff - turning his failings into pseudo-intellectual justifications.

jokes.text: I thought you should break into groups, but you failed to learn that, so your theory's invalid.
jokes.setup: Jeff being challenged on his teaching philosophy
jokes.punchline: Jeff using circular logic to 'win' argument
jokes.explanation: Brilliant lawyer logic applied to teaching - complete BS that sounds convincing

jokes.text: You don't argue with Annie, Garrett, you let her argue with herself until she loses.
jokes.setup: Garrett asking how Jeff won an argument with Annie
jokes.punchline: Jeff reveals his strategy of non-engagement leading to self-defeat
jokes.explanation: Brilliant insight into both Jeff's cunning and Annie's overthinking. Very quotable wisdom.

jokes.text: Because they draw a circle around something called 'The Truth,' and they say that everything outside it is a lie.
jokes.setup: Jeff explaining legal strategy to students
jokes.punchline: Philosophical insight about how lawyers manipulate truth concepts
jokes.explanation: Deep character moment showing Jeff's actual legal insight and cynicism about truth.

jokes.text: He gave you a dead rat? - No, this is my witness intimidation project.
jokes.setup: Jeff assumes the rat is random cruelty
jokes.punchline: Annie reveals it's actually coursework - making it worse somehow
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - the reality is somehow more disturbing than the assumption.

jokes.text: Because this... this is my reality, this is how I learned to be, and my being doesn't allow for Nicolas... Freaking Cage, okay?
jokes.setup: Abed trying to explain his analytical breakdown
jokes.punchline: His usual articulate analysis dissolves into frustrated incoherence
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment - Abed's analytical framework completely breaking down.

jokes.text: Oh, I'm a cat. I'm a sexy cat.
jokes.setup: Abed's mental breakdown from Nicolas Cage analysis
jokes.punchline: Random Cage-like behavior - acting like a sexy cat
jokes.explanation: Peak absurdist moment. Abed channeling Cage's random energy perfectly.

jokes.text: Think of something safe like Holly Hunter, or Don Cheadle!
jokes.setup: Annie trying to help Abed recover from Cage overload
jokes.punchline: Suggests 'safe' actors who are predictably good
jokes.explanation: Good choices of 'safe' actors that contrast with Cage's unpredictability.

jokes.text: That was brilliant.
jokes.setup: Abed just had complete mental breakdown acting like animals
jokes.punchline: Professor treats it as successful academic performance
jokes.explanation: Good irony - professor completely misreading Abed's genuine crisis as performance art.

jokes.text: Are you drawing a peanut? - It's a duck, and it's none of your concern, actually.
jokes.setup: Jeff questioning Hickey's artistic abilities
jokes.punchline: Hickey defensively insists it's a duck and gets territorial about it
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Hickey's artistic pretensions and defensiveness.

jokes.text: I was in the storm drain lair of the Black River Ripper. I have seen human heads used for things other than heads.
jokes.setup: Jeff calling Hickey a monster for giving Annie an A-minus
jokes.punchline: Hickey's disturbing criminology experience puts grading in perspective
jokes.explanation: Perfectly dark Hickey backstory. 'Things other than heads' is brilliantly vague and disturbing.

jokes.text: I may hook up with it again a few more times even if I do dump it right before Christmas.
jokes.setup: Jeff comparing his relationship with teaching to dating
jokes.punchline: Describes typical Jeff relationship pattern - casual use then abandonment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jeff metaphor - treats everything like a casual relationship to be discarded.

jokes.text: It's one duck, his name is Jim, and publishers are interested!
jokes.setup: Jeff insulting Hickey's cartooning ability
jokes.punchline: Hickey's defensive response reveals absurd specificity and delusion
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation. Jim the duck and publisher interest are perfectly delusional details.

jokes.text: An A-minus means you actually earned an 'A,' but the teacher doesn't like you.
jokes.setup: Annie confused about her grade change
jokes.punchline: Jeff reveals the arbitrary, petty nature of minus grades
jokes.explanation: Reveals the corrupt system behind academic grading. Good callback to Hickey's earlier explanation.

jokes.text: Et tu, Brute? Am I using that right?
jokes.setup: Annie feeling betrayed by the grading conspiracy
jokes.punchline: Self-consciously checks if she's using the classical reference correctly
jokes.explanation: Good Annie moment - even in betrayal, she's concerned about academic accuracy.

jokes.text: Minuses are made up! It's riot time! Yeah! Bring me their heads!
jokes.setup: Annie discovering academic conspiracy
jokes.punchline: Annie immediately inciting violent revolution
jokes.explanation: Great escalation showing Annie's transformation from good student to revolutionary

jokes.text: Pop pop!
jokes.setup: Student riot in progress
jokes.punchline: Magnitude's signature catchphrase in revolutionary context
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective use of established character trait in new context.

jokes.text: Pop pop!
jokes.setup: Riot atmosphere
jokes.punchline: Magnitude's signature catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Classic running gag, not original but perfectly placed and character-appropriate

jokes.text: I thought the meaning of people was somewhere in here. Then I looked inside Nicolas Cage and I found a secret. People are random and pointless.
jokes.setup: Abed explaining his existential crisis from Nicolas Cage analysis
jokes.punchline: Cage study led to nihilistic revelation about human nature
jokes.explanation: Deep Abed character work - his analytical nature leading to existential crisis via pop culture.

jokes.text: Prophets, messiahs, kung fu pandas.
jokes.setup: Shirley explaining people who challenge your understanding
jokes.punchline: Including animated movie character in list with religious figures
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation that somehow makes perfect sense while being completely absurd

jokes.text: So Nicolas Cage is Jesus? - Uh, no, but he clearly works in mysterious ways.
jokes.setup: Abed processing Shirley's religious metaphor about Cage
jokes.punchline: Shirley carefully clarifies Cage isn't Jesus while maintaining the parallel
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - Shirley's religious perspective applied to pop culture absurdly but helpfully.

jokes.text: A demon to some and an angel to others, like a cenobite.
jokes.setup: Abed working through Cage's dual nature
jokes.punchline: References Hellraiser demons who are simultaneously pleasure and pain
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abed reference - obscure horror metaphor that actually illuminates the discussion.

jokes.text: You like Hellraiser? You know the word 'Hellraiser'? I thought pinhead in space was a bit of a letdown, but I'm in for the ride.
jokes.setup: Abed surprised Shirley knows horror references
jokes.punchline: Shirley reveals deep knowledge and opinions about horror sequels
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of Shirley's character - revealing unexpected horror movie expertise.

jokes.text: Do you think the students and teachers at Harvard are at each other's throats? No, because they're all rich.
jokes.setup: Jeff trying to unite students and teachers during riot
jokes.punchline: Reduces all conflict to economic class differences
jokes.explanation: Good class-conscious observation from Jeff. Typical cynical but accurate insight.

jokes.text: We all just went through an entire week of meatball lunches without even blinking. - Oh, that's on me. I converted the lunch menu to a spreadsheet.
jokes.setup: Jeff pointing out their shared suffering with cafeteria food
jokes.punchline: Dean proudly takes credit for making it worse through Excel optimization
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dean moment - bureaucratic optimization making everything worse.

jokes.text: Those of us that get to leave aren't going anywhere.
jokes.setup: Jeff trying to unite the Greendale community
jokes.punchline: Points out the ironic truth - no one escapes Greendale
jokes.explanation: Profound and darkly funny truth about the show's universe. Very economical delivery.

jokes.text: I think that's why they call it 'Excel.'
jokes.setup: Dean explaining Excel is based on rows and columns of cells
jokes.punchline: Thinks the name comes from cells, not excellence
jokes.explanation: Classic Dean misunderstanding - completely wrong but delivered with confidence.

jokes.text: And don't forget, if I fire you, you're likely starve and die, so you know.
jokes.setup: Dean trying to motivate Jeff to stay involved
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions Jeff's economic vulnerability and potential death
jokes.explanation: Dean's casual threat delivered with cheerful matter-of-factness.

jokes.text: There are two things I don't do well, apologies and drawing duck bills.
jokes.setup: Hickey preparing to apologize to Jeff
jokes.punchline: Admits two very different personal failings with equal importance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hickey priorities - equating artistic and emotional shortcomings. Great callback.

jokes.text: I'm taste-testing rations for the shelter I'm digging.
jokes.setup: Hickey eating nuts at committee meeting
jokes.punchline: Casually reveals he's preparing for apocalypse with underground shelter
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hickey paranoia delivered as casual small talk.

jokes.text: I'm taste-testing rations for the shelter I'm digging.
jokes.setup: Buzz eating something unappetizing
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning apocalypse preparation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Buzz throwaway line showing his paranoid preparedness

jokes.text: I would like to address the fact that there are no Asian-Americans represented here. - We can get Chang. - No need to rush into anything.
jokes.setup: Annie noting lack of Asian-American representation
jokes.punchline: Immediately backpedals when Chang is suggested as solution
jokes.explanation: Good commentary on tokenism vs. actual representation. Chang's reputation precedes him.

jokes.text: What do you mean my insurance won't cover it anymore? My body doesn't respond well to the generic stuff.
jokes.setup: Students pranking Hickey by listening to his private call
jokes.punchline: Real vulnerability about healthcare and medication costs
jokes.explanation: Dark turn - prank reveals real economic hardship. More poignant than funny.

jokes.text: I'm an educator, ma'am, living on a teacher's salary!
jokes.setup: Hickey trying to afford medication
jokes.punchline: Frustrated explanation of teacher economic reality
jokes.explanation: Darkly comic but mostly sad - real commentary on teacher economic struggles.

jokes.text: I wake up every night screaming. Life is unfair, but it's the only thing we g...
jokes.setup: Hickey's insurance call getting increasingly desperate
jokes.punchline: Reveals night terrors and existential despair before being cut off
jokes.explanation: Dark character depth - Hickey's PTSD and philosophy revealed in insurance desperation.

jokes.text: We can't afford to bury dad with the rest of the family.
jokes.setup: Students expecting more prank call content
jokes.punchline: Hickey's genuinely heartbreaking call to his mother about funeral costs
jokes.explanation: Devastating end - prank reveals genuine poverty and family tragedy. More heartbreaking than funny.

tmdb_overview: Abed tackles a two-day crash course on Nicolas Cage, while a clueless Jeff begins his teaching career with Annie as his most demanding student.
insight: Teaching metahumor sustains 81-point episode through character-driven absurdism.
